auto_posts.title: Azure Local Update — February 5, 2026
auto_posts.subtitle: Documentation changes
auto_posts.content:
## Azure Local Documentation Updates: Key Terminology and Process Changes

Microsoft has standardized terminology across Azure Local documentation, changing references from generic "Azure Local" to "Azure Local instance" when referring to specific deployments. The SQL Server deployment guide now explicitly lists supported workloads including "Online transaction processing (OLTP) workloads" rather than using the acronym inline.

**Action Required:** - Update internal runbooks to reference "Azure Local instance" instead of generic "Azure Local" when documenting specific deployments - VM creation now supports three methods: Azure CLI, Azure portal, and ARM templates (previously "Azure Resource Manager template") - RBAC references now explicitly state "Azure role-based access control" on first use

These changes improve precision in multi-instance environments where distinguishing between the platform and specific instances matters for operational clarity.

📖 Read the updated documentation →

auto_posts.title: Azure Local Update — February 5, 2026
auto_posts.subtitle: Documentation changes
auto_posts.content:
The latest Azure Local documentation update streamlines deployment workflows for Kubernetes clusters and SQL Server workloads. The AKS Arc cluster creation guide now emphasizes Azure Arc connectivity by default and clarifies Microsoft Entra group requirements for administrator access - a critical change for teams managing cluster permissions. The portal-based deployment process has been reorganized with clearer prerequisite sections, replacing the previous checklist format with explicit requirement statements.

SQL Server deployment documentation received significant updates with 14 additional lines covering enhanced configuration details. The virtual machine creation guide underwent major restructuring, maintaining content while improving navigation flow. Prerequisites across all deployment scenarios now include expanded subscription and sign-in procedures, with 8 new lines added to VM prerequisites alone. Operations teams should review these updated workflows before their next deployment, particularly the modified Microsoft Entra group configuration steps for Kubernetes clusters, as the access control model now requires explicit group membership for kubectl access.

View full commit on GitHub →

auto_posts.content:
Azure Local deployment documentation has been updated to reflect the latest v1.2.3 release. Key changes include improved prerequisite documentation and additional guidance for cluster operators. This update simplifies the deployment experience and reduces common configuration errors.
